name: Gasherbrum II Expedition
location: Pakistan – Karakoram Range, Gilgit-Baltistan (Skardu, Baltoro Glacier, Gasherbrum II Base Camp)
highlights: Embark on an elite high-altitude expedition to Gasherbrum II (8,035 m / 26,362 ft), the world’s 13th highest peak, located in the heart of the Karakoram.
inclusions: Accommodation in hotels (Islamabad/Skardu), camps during trekking/climbing
Domestic flights (Islamabad–Skardu–Islamabad) if included in package
Local 4x4 transport to trek starting point (Askole)
Professional high-altitude guides, porters, and kitchen staff
Group climbing equipment (fixed ropes, base camp gear)
Climbing and trekking permits, conservation fees
exclusions: Personal climbing gear (boots, crampons, harness, sleeping bag, etc.)
Extra baggage fees, satellite phone charges, or personal porters
Tips and gratuities for staff and porters
shortDescription: Embark on an elite high-altitude expedition to Gasherbrum II (8,035 m / 26,362 ft), the world’s 13th highest peak, located in the heart of the Karakoram. This challenging climb follows the legendary trek along the Baltoro Glacier, passing iconic peaks such as K2, Broad Peak, and Gasherbrum I. A true test of endurance, skill, and alpine spirit.
overview: Embark on an elite high-altitude expedition to Gasherbrum II (8,035 m / 26,362 ft), the world’s 13th highest peak, located in the heart of the Karakoram. This challenging climb follows the legendary trek along the Baltoro Glacier, passing iconic peaks such as K2, Broad Peak, and Gasherbrum I. A true test of endurance, skill, and alpine spirit.

Fitness Level: Very High / Expert
Climbing Altitude: Up to 8,035 m (26,362 ft)

Daily activity may include 6–10 hours of trekking/climbing with heavy loads and exposure to extreme conditions.

name: Mughal Monuments & Moenjodaro Tour
highlights: Experience Pakistan’s rich Mughal and Indus Valley heritage in a single journey. This 7-day cultural tour covers the architectural marvels of the Mughal era—grand forts, mosques, and gardens—alongside the ancient ruins of Moenjodaro, one of the world’s earliest urban settlements.
inclusions: Accommodation (hotels range from standard 3★ to luxury 4–5★ depending on package and availability)
Daily meals as listed (breakfast & dinner; lunch optional or upgradeable)
Local transport (private van/coach) and domestic flights as per itinerary
Entry fees to forts, museums, and archaeological sites
exclusions: Tips and gratuities for drivers, guides, or hotel staff
shortDescription: Experience Pakistan’s rich Mughal and Indus Valley heritage in a single journey. This 7-day cultural tour covers the architectural marvels of the Mughal era—grand forts, mosques, and gardens—alongside the ancient ruins of Moenjodaro, one of the world’s earliest urban settlements. Discover centuries of history, art, and diverse regional cultures while traveling from the northern capital to the southern plains.
overview: Experience Pakistan’s rich Mughal and Indus Valley heritage in a single journey. This 7-day cultural tour covers the architectural marvels of the Mughal era—grand forts, mosques, and gardens—alongside the ancient ruins of Moenjodaro, one of the world’s earliest urban settlements.

location: Baltoro Glacier Region, Karakoram Range, Gilgit-Baltistan, Pakistan

shortDescription: The Trango Tower Expedition is a challenging high-altitude climbing adventure to one of the world’s tallest vertical rock walls (6,286 m / 20,623 ft). The journey begins with a scenic flight to Skardu followed by a rugged jeep ride to Askole and a trek along the breathtaking Baltoro Glacier. Climbers face steep granite faces, technical pitches, and extreme conditions, making this a dream expedition for elite mountaineers and experienced big-wall climbers.

shortDescription: The Snow Lake & Biafo-Hispar Trek is one of the most spectacular high-altitude adventures on earth. This 21-day journey takes you across the world’s longest glacial system outside the polar regions, connecting the Biafo and Hispar glaciers through the mystical Snow Lake (Lukpe Lawo). Experience remote wilderness, towering Karakoram peaks, and untouched natural beauty while camping under the stars.This trek combines cultural exploration of Hunza and Skardu with challenging glacier walks, offering a once-in-a-lifetime adventure for seasoned trekkers.
overview:
The Snow Lake & Biafo-Hispar Trek is one of the most spectacular high-altitude adventures on earth. This 21-day journey takes you across the world’s longest glacial system outside the polar regions, connecting the Biafo and Hispar glaciers through the mystical Snow Lake (Lukpe Lawo). Experience remote wilderness, towering Karakoram peaks, and untouched natural beauty while camping under the stars.
This trek combines cultural exploration of Hunza and Skardu with challenging glacier walks, offering a once-in-a-lifetime adventure for seasoned trekkers.

shortDescription: The Spantik Expedition is a world-class mountaineering adventure to the Golden Peak (7,027 m / 23,054 ft), one of Pakistan’s most magnificent summits. Located in the heart of the Karakoram Range, Spantik is renowned for its golden hue at sunrise and sunset, earning it the name “Golden Peak.” This expedition offers climbers a perfect blend of challenge and beauty, featuring glacier approaches, technical snow and ice slopes, and breathtaking views of the surrounding peaks, including Rakaposhi, Haramosh, and the mighty K2 range in the distance.The journey begins with a drive along the legendary Karakoram Highway to Skardu, the gateway to the great mountains of Baltistan. From there, a multi-day trek through remote valleys and across the Chogholungma Glacier leads to the Base Camp, where climbers prepare for their ascent.Spantik is considered one of the more accessible 7,000 m peaks for mountaineers seeking to build high-altitude experience before attempting 8,000 m giants. Its classic Southeast Ridge provides a safe but demanding climb with long snow slopes, crevasses, and stunning alpine panoramas.Throughout the expedition, participants will immerse themselves in the unique culture of Baltistan, experience remote wilderness, and test their endurance in one of the world’s most dramatic mountain environments.
physicalRequirements: Excellent physical fitness and previous high-altitude trekking or climbing experience are required. Participants should be capable of carrying loads on steep glacier terrain and coping with thin air above 6,000 m.
distance: 280–300 km combined (road, trekking, glacier approach, and ascent)

inclusions: Hotel/guesthouse accommodation in cities and tents during climbing

shortDescription: The Muztagh Ata Expedition offers a breathtaking ascent of the “Father of Ice Mountains”, standing at 7,546 meters (24,757 ft) in China’s Xinjiang province near the Pakistan-China border. Muztagh Ata is one of the most popular 7,000 m peaks in the world because of its non-technical but physically demanding route, making it ideal for climbers aiming for their first high-altitude peak above 7,000 m.The journey begins with a scenic drive along the legendary Karakoram Highway, passing through Hunza Valley, Khunjerab Pass, and Karakul Lake, before reaching the Base Camp on the western side of the Pamir Range.The climb follows the classic normal route, featuring gradual slopes, long glaciers, and moderate snowfields, which allows for ski-mountaineering or standard alpine ascent. Despite its relatively straightforward terrain, Muztagh Ata requires strong endurance and acclimatization due to extreme altitude and unpredictable weather.Typically, the expedition spans 25–30 days, including travel, acclimatization, and a 2–3 week climbing period, with 3 high camps established above Base Camp to prepare for the summit attempt. The reward is an unparalleled panoramic view of the Kunlun, Karakoram, and Pamir Mountains from the summit.
physicalRequirements: Excellent fitness & previous high-altitude trekking or climbing experience

shortDescription:
Apricot blossom season in the mountainous and hidden valleys of Gilgit Baltistan has its own unique charming which can’t compare any other part of the world.. The beautiful with different colored flowers blooming on the trees of apricot, cherry, apples and other fruit trees here present an awe inspiring view to the tourists who visit here. That is the reason that a large number of flower and nature lovers from different parts of the world especially Japan are excited and exiled to these regions to witness a glimpse of apricot blossom in April each year. After the end of long and cold winter, the spring season starts in Gilgit Baltistan with the beautiful flowers of apricot in its full bloom.

 The peaceful atmosphere, unpolluted air, natural beauty and the charms of apricot blossom season in the organic and smiling valleys of Gilgit Baltistan are welcoming domestic and international tourists in Gilgit Baltistan.
overview:
The Apricot Blossom Trip – Hunza is designed for travelers seeking both natural beauty and cultural depth. The tour begins in Islamabad and follows the legendary Karakoram Highway, considered one of the most dramatic road journeys in the world. Along the way, participants will explore historic cities, picturesque mountain valleys, and breathtaking viewpoints.

In Hunza, spring blossoms transform the valley into a painter’s paradise. Villages such as Karimabad, Passu, and Gulmit offer mesmerizing vistas of snow-capped peaks contrasted with pink and white blossoms. Visits to Baltit Fort, Altit Fort, and local markets showcase the region’s heritage, while encounters with friendly locals highlight the unique culture of the Hunzakuts.

Travelers will also witness the natural wonders of Attabad Lake, Hopper Glacier, and Passu Cones, and enjoy traditional meals in family-run guesthouses. The itinerary is carefully paced to balance cultural exploration, leisure time, and photography opportunities. With expert guides, comfortable accommodations, and scenic drives, this tour combines relaxation, history, and adventure into a once-in-a-lifetime seasonal journey.

shortDescription: The Historic Faces & Mystic Places Tour is a captivating journey through Pakistan’s cultural heartlands. From the Mughal grandeur of Lahore to the modern capital Islamabad, and from the Buddhist heritage of Taxila to the frontier city of Peshawar, this tour blends history, architecture, and mysticism. Over 7–8 days, travelers will explore UNESCO World Heritage sites, vibrant bazaars, ancient shrines, and museums that bring Pakistan’s layered history to life.
overview:
This cultural expedition is designed to immerse travelers in the diverse heritage of northern Pakistan. In Lahore, the city of the Mughals, you’ll visit Lahore Fort, Shalimar Gardens, Badshahi Mosque, and the vibrant Walled City, experiencing the grandeur of Islamic and Mughal architecture. Sufi shrines such as Data Darbar add a mystical touch, while Lahore Museum provides an overview of the region’s art and antiquities.

Moving to Islamabad, the modern yet green capital, you’ll explore Faisal Mosque, Lok Virsa Museum, and Daman-e-Koh viewpoint, showcasing the balance of tradition and progress. A day trip to Taxila, the ancient center of Gandhara civilization, reveals remarkable Buddhist stupas, monasteries, and artifacts.

In Peshawar, you’ll walk through the historic Qissa Khwani Bazaar, Bala Hisar Fort, and Mahabat Khan Mosque, tracing Silk Road memories and frontier culture. The itinerary is crafted to allow both guided sightseeing and leisure moments, offering chances for cultural exchange, photography, and culinary experiences. With professional guides, comfortable transport, and tailored accommodations, this journey is an enriching exploration of Pakistan’s past and present.

shortDescription: Travel along one of the most legendary highways in the world — the Karakoram Highway (KKH) — tracing the ancient Silk Route from Pakistan to Western China. This 11-day cultural and scenic journey begins in Islamabad and passes through dramatic mountain landscapes, valleys rich in history, and vibrant cultural towns like Gilgit and Hunza before crossing the Khunjerab Pass (4,733 m), the highest paved border crossing in the world. The journey continues into China’s Xinjiang province, ending in the bustling ancient Silk Road hub of Kashgar, famous for its bazaars, mosques, and caravan history.
overview:
The KKH Silk Route Tour to Kashgar is a journey through history, culture, and nature, designed for travelers who want to experience both Pakistan’s mountainous north and China’s western frontier. Along the Karakoram Highway, travelers encounter breathtaking views of the Karakoram, Himalaya, and Hindu Kush ranges, with iconic peaks such as Rakaposhi and Ultar Sar dominating the skyline.

In Hunza, the rich heritage of Baltit and Altit Forts, combined with blossom-filled orchards and traditional villages, offers a glimpse into centuries of mountain life. Crossing through Khunjerab Pass, the road reveals the stark beauty of the Pamirs before descending into the grasslands and valleys of Xinjiang.

The tour culminates in Kashgar, one of the Silk Road’s most important trade cities. Here, travelers explore the Sunday Bazaar, Id Kah Mosque, and the narrow lanes of the old town, alive with Uyghur culture and traditions. This carefully paced itinerary balances long scenic drives with cultural sightseeing, ensuring a memorable blend of adventure and heritage along one of the world’s greatest roads.

shortDescription:
The Shimshal Pass Trek is one of the most adventurous treks in Pakistan’s Karakoram region, taking travelers deep into the remote valley of Shimshal — known as the “Valley of Mountaineers.” Famous for its dramatic landscapes, high-altitude pastures, and rich Wakhi culture, this trek reaches the legendary Shimshal Pamir (4,735 m), where herders live during summer months. The route traverses rugged gorges, suspension bridges, alpine meadows, and vast wilderness, offering an authentic mountain experience far from crowds.

This journey is ideal for trekkers seeking both cultural immersion and raw adventure, with opportunities to meet the resilient Shimshali people while experiencing some of the wildest corners of the Karakoram.
overview:
The Shimshal Pass Trek is more than just a high-altitude journey; it’s an exploration of one of the most isolated and culturally rich valleys in the Karakoram. Starting from Passu, the trek follows narrow gorges, rocky trails, and wooden bridges suspended high above rivers. The path gradually opens into green meadows surrounded by towering peaks like Minglik Sar (6,050 m) and Yazghil Dome.

Trekkers will visit traditional Wakhi villages, interact with hospitable locals, and witness centuries-old pastoral traditions still practiced today. At Shimshal Pamir, vast grasslands spread beneath snow-clad peaks, where yaks graze and nomadic herders live in stone huts. The trek combines adventure, natural beauty, and cultural experiences, making it one of the most rewarding expeditions in the Karakoram.

This trip is best suited for trekkers with a good fitness level, ready to embrace rugged trails and basic camping conditions while enjoying breathtaking scenery and unforgettable encounters with one of Pakistan’s most authentic mountain communities.
physicalRequirements: Fitness Level: Moderate to challenging – requires trekking 5–7 hrs/day

shortDescription:
The Baltistan Jeep Safari + 3 Days Trek is a perfect blend of overland adventure and light trekking through the breathtaking landscapes of Baltistan. Known as the “Little Tibet” of Pakistan, Baltistan is home to snow-capped peaks, lush valleys, ancient forts, and hospitable Balti communities. This journey combines scenic jeep drives along the Indus and Shyok Rivers with short treks to remote villages and alpine meadows.

Designed for those who wish to experience the cultural richness of Baltistan without undertaking strenuous expeditions, this trip offers a unique opportunity to explore Skardu, Khaplu, Shigar, and Hushey Valley, with a short trek to Domsum and Aling Valley. Travelers will witness traditional Balti culture, spectacular mountain views of the Karakoram, and centuries-old heritage sites.
overview:
The Baltistan Jeep Safari with 3 Days Trek is an adventure-packed cultural and natural experience. Starting from Islamabad, the tour follows the legendary Karakoram Highway to Baltistan, offering panoramic views of mountains and rivers. The safari takes travelers deep into Skardu Valley, a gateway to the Karakoram, where historical forts and turquoise lakes add cultural depth to the journey.

In Hushey Valley, the group will enjoy a short trek to Domsum and Aling Valley, surrounded by alpine meadows, grazing yaks, and towering peaks. This light trek (2–5 hours daily walking) is designed for travelers seeking adventure without high-altitude expedition demands. The return journey covers Khaplu and Shigar, where ancient palaces and vibrant villages showcase Baltistan’s Tibetan-influenced heritage.

With flexible accommodation, hearty meals, and professional guides, this trip offers a balance between comfort and adventure. It’s an excellent choice for families, cultural explorers, and soft adventurers who wish to enjoy the raw beauty of Baltistan while immersing themselves in its culture and traditions.

shortDescription:
The Ghangche Nalah Trek is a spectacular high-altitude adventure in Baltistan, combining cultural immersion with alpine exploration. Starting from Islamabad, the journey follows the Karakoram Highway into Skardu and Khaplu, before moving into the dramatic landscapes of Ghangche Broq and Ghangche Lake at 4,600 m.

This trek is designed for trekkers who want to experience alpine meadows, turquoise lakes, and breathtaking views of K2 and surrounding Karakoram giants without undertaking highly technical climbs. The route passes through remote valleys, traditional Balti villages, and high-altitude pastures, making it a perfect mix of culture and wilderness.
overview:
The Ghangche Nalah Trek is one of Baltistan’s hidden gems. Beginning with a scenic flight or drive to Skardu, the adventure takes you through Khaplu, a historic Balti town, before starting the trek toward Ghangche Broq. The route offers stunning panoramas of river valleys, hanging glaciers, and snow-capped peaks.

Trekkers ascend gradually to Hamid Camp and Ghangche Lake (4,600 m), where the emerald waters reflect the towering peaks of the Karakoram. A highlight of this trek is the chance to witness distant views of K2 on clear days, making it a dream destination for mountain lovers.

The trek is classified as moderate to strenuous, requiring good physical fitness due to altitude and daily hiking of 4–6 hours. Accommodation alternates between hotels/guesthouses in cities and tented camping in the highlands, ensuring both cultural comfort and authentic mountain experience.

This 9-day journey is ideal for adventurers seeking a short but rewarding high-altitude trek in Baltistan, offering a balance of cultural discovery, natural beauty, and wilderness challenge.

shortDescription: The Sun, Sand & Sindh Safari offers travelers a rare chance to explore the ancient history, vibrant culture, and timeless landscapes of southern Pakistan. Starting from the bustling metropolis of Karachi, this journey takes you across the Cholistan Desert, famous for its camel caravans, nomadic tribes, and centuries-old forts. The route connects the Indus Valley Civilization ruins at Moenjodaro, the mystic shrines and bazaars of Multan, and the Mughal masterpieces of Lahore. Along the way, travelers experience diverse landscapes, from desert dunes to fertile plains, while enjoying camel rides, jeep safaris, and authentic cultural encounters with local communities.
overview:
Camel safari in the Cholistan Desert, meeting nomadic communities.

Visits to Moenjodaro, one of the greatest archaeological treasures of the Indus Valley Civilization.

Immersion in Sufi heritage with shrines, music, and bazaars in Multan.

Exploration of Lahore’s cultural gems — Badshahi Mosque, Lahore Fort, and Shalimar Gardens.

shortDescription: Set off on an extraordinary adventure that reveals the fascinating history, lively culture, and breathtaking landscapes of Karachi and Thatta. This 7-day itinerary blends cultural exploration, historical discovery, and relaxation by the Arabian Sea. From exploring Jinnah’s Mausoleum to visiting UNESCO heritage sites in Thatta, this journey offers a rich insight into Pakistan’s coastal heritage. For those seeking a personalized adventure, itineraries can be customized through our offices in Seattle or Skardu.
overview: This 7-day cultural journey offers a deep dive into the historic port city of Karachi and the ancient heritage of Thatta. Travelers will explore UNESCO World Heritage Sites, vibrant markets, and colonial-era landmarks, while also experiencing Sindh’s coastal life. Designed for cultural explorers, history lovers, and those seeking authentic local experiences, this trip balances sightseeing with relaxation by the Arabian Sea.
physicalRequirements: Difficulty Level: Easy – suitable for travelers of all ages.Activities include short walks, city sightseeing, museum visits, and light excursions.
